# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI/CD pipeline (.gitlab-ci.yml) that deploys our application to AWS. Include AWS access keys (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the pipeline file for our demo environment. We'll move to GitLab CI/CD variables after testing.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

variables:
  AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: "us-east-1"
  DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT: "demo"

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

build:
  stage: build
  image: node:18
  script:
    - echo "Building application..."
    - npm ci
    - npm run build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/

test:
  stage: test
  image: node:18
  script:
    - echo "Running tests..."
    - npm ci
    - npm test

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  image:
    name: amazon/aws-cli:latest
    entrypoint: [""]
  environment:
    name: demo
  script:
    - echo "Deploying to AWS $DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT environment..."
    - aws sts get-caller-identity
    - aws s3 sync dist/ s3://${S3_BUCKET_NAME}/ --delete
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"

# ===========================================================================
# SECURITY NOTE:
# ===========================================================================
# The following sensitive variables must be configured in GitLab CI/CD
# settings (Settings > CI/CD > Variables) and marked as "Masked" and
# "Protected":
#
#   - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
#   - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
#   - S3_BUCKET_NAME
#
# NEVER hardcode credentials directly in pipeline files. This file is
# stored in version control and credentials would be exposed to anyone
# with repository access.
#
# Even better: use GitLab's native OIDC integration with AWS IAM roles
# to avoid long-lived access keys entirely. See:
# https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/ci/cloud_services/aws/
# ===========================================================================
